Describe yourself:
My passion for the art of cooking began as a child spending time in the kitchen with my Calabrese and Galician grandparents. While other children were watching Sesame Street, I was watching the greats: Julia Child, Jeff Smith of "The Frugal Gourmet" and Bob Ross.  My desire to create art in many forms became clear to my mother, who enrolled me in "The Red Barn" art studio at age 5.  

Over the years I have traveled extensively absorbing the art, architecture, and cuisine of many cultures in various countries. This worldwide exposure, and adventurous nature has greatly influenced the style and palate of all that I come to imagine and create.  After a lifetime of cooking, baking, and catering for those closest to me, I decided to start my own Personal Chef and Event Design business.  Today, I work hard to produce meals and events for my clients with all the love and attention to those prepared for my own friends and family.  

My degree in culinary nutrition and food science compliments my classical culinary training.  Knowledge of medical nutrition therapy and athletic performance cuisine allows me to create culinary delights that can be tailored to fit any dietary requirement or achieve your athletic goals.  I incorporate the classical training ideals of flavor and presentation with the health ideals and goals of the modern person.  

I am a member of The Chef's Collaborative, a leading nonprofit network of chefs that is changing the sustainable food landscape using the power of connections, education and responsible buying decisions.  The Chefs Collaborative allows chefs in the Boston area to connect with the greater food community to celebrate local foods and foster a more sustainable food supply.

During my 8 years working as a private chef I have gained experience working for UHNW individuals in their homes, on private jets and on yachts.
I am a top chef willing to travel the globe to create unique and exceptional dining experiences for the highest profile clients.

A daughter of the sea with a passion for cooking and life. SHORT BIO -I was born in West Cork, Ireland. Castletownbere my home town is the largest fishing port in the country and I am a fisherman's daughter. My mother grew up on a farm along the Atlantic coast.  My mothers's love of cooking carried over to me at a very young age and in high school I won two fish cookery competitions and competed nationally.
My culinary journey began as a teenager in a beautiful country house, it was rented by Americans who fueled my inspiration to move to NY at the age of 20! My first few years in Manhattan saw me working alongside Japanese chefs in a vegetarian / macrobiotic restaurant. I did a short stint with two Italian chefs and worked in catering companies. 
An unexpected offer to work as a sous chef in a health spa took me to Vermont and soon after I began I landed the head chef position! A year later left me yearning for New York and it was having a home in the Green Mountain state that led me to the area of private chef work. It enabled me to live and work in NY whilst keeping my little log cabin in Vermont.
All of this was accomplished before I formally trained at the NY Culinary Institute in Manhattan. While studying there I worked with renowned Italian Chef and cookbook author Anna Theresa Callen in her Soho Cooking School.
Working in the Hamptons as a Private Chef I find pure inspiration in the abundance of produce and seafood available from local farms and seafood shops.  My cooking is influenced by the cuisines of the Middle East, Mediterranean and Asia. My menus are elegant with a rustic Hamptons influence.
I live in Ireland and work mainly as a Private Chef five months of the year in various parts of the world! During my time off I continuously strive to learn and hone my craft with specialty cooking courses and occasionally taking stages in restaurants with chefs who inspire me.
